findings.message: loadItemsQuietly() catches fetchItems() failures and returns [] with no rethrow and no telemetry. The prior code set error state (role="alert") on failure; the new code drops the .catch path entirely, so a failed item load now renders as an empty list. The failure reaches neither the window 'error' handler nor the RUM js-errors counter (POST /internal/rum), making backend/API load failures completely invisible to monitoring — fetch errors will show as silent zero-result views rather than alertable error signals.
findings.suggestion: Do not swallow the rejection. Either rethrow after reporting (so the window 'error'/unhandledrejection handler and js-errors counter fire), or explicitly increment the RUM error signal before returning a fallback — e.g. report to the FrontendMetrics beacon and keep setError(true) so the failure is both user-visible and counted. Silent return [] hides a real outage.
